text:
I was just a young fox, frolicking through the forest. I loved exploring, discovering new sights and sounds, and making new friends. One bright afternoon, while scampering through a copse of maple trees, I found myself staring into a small crack between the branches.

To my astonishment, there lay a curious-looking creature with big, floppy ears and a voice that echoed through the trees like a melodious siren. It wore little more than a patchwork quilted robe made from old pillowcases. Curiously, I ventured closer, cautiously peering through the leafiest branches.

The creature beckoned with its brow-raised paw. I approached slowly, fascinated. That's when it spoke—not with words, but with a melody that swarmed me with sweet whispers. "Friend?"

I gaped, then shook my head. "I don’t speak—

"It's okay," it finished, with a giggle that made a squirrel dance madly. "You speak our language
